examples.title: STRIPS Operator Definition
examples.description: Representation of a planning operator with preconditions and effects
examples.explanation: This defines a stack operator in blocks world. Preconditions require holding the block and the target being clear. Effects add new facts (block on target, hand empty) and delete old facts (no longer holding block, target no longer clear).

examples.title: Forward State-Space Search
examples.explanation: Starts from initial state, iteratively applies actions forward until goal is reached. Uses a frontier (queue/stack) to manage nodes. Applicable actions are those whose preconditions are satisfied in current state.

examples.title: Backward State-Space Search (Regression)
examples.explanation: Starts from goal state and works backward using goal regression. For each goal, finds actions that can achieve it and computes what must be true before that action. More focused but regression can be complex.

examples.title: GraphPlan Algorithm
examples.description: Two-phase planning algorithm using planning graphs
examples.explanation: GraphPlan has two phases: graph expansion builds increasingly detailed planning graphs until goals appear at the same level without mutex conflicts. Solution extraction performs backward search through the graph to find a valid plan.

examples.title: Partial-Order Planning (POP)
examples.description: Algorithm for building partially ordered plans
examples.explanation: POP builds plans incrementally by selecting open preconditions and finding actions to satisfy them. It maintains causal links showing which action achieves which precondition. Threats are resolved through promotion or demotion of threatening actions.
